ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Na alɛ­mbaa ni nbem atɔtɔ lɔlɔɛ nu, na lɔlɔɛ n anyi akata i ŋu, nyini ti, wɔ koro awura má mbaa.
translated: And Alɛmbaa ate my food there, and his food was eaten by the dog, then he went to the village elder to get help.

ref: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
text: Na Yesu ase bu ka, “Am ma nbaatumam n bra m dɔ, má am fɔn bu. Dama bɛrɛ ŋusu sɔ ye Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti bu dɛɛ a.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, "You have the knowledge of the way of God, but you did not enter. Similarly, the tax collectors and sinners and prostitutes entered the kingdom of God before you."

ref: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
text: Saŋga bo Nyɛmɛ sa­yoo­wa dɔŋgu­yo­fɔ nkpiɛn­kpiɛnm, ni Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm n ŋuuri sam n bo i yoori bu ni, ni kabo nbaatumam bo bu woori Nyɛmɛ Seree­biri Awuru n nu bo tuntuunri i ka, “Hosana. Ya kansi Fɛmɛ Davidi anuma ni,” na yaa ati bu.
translated: "Sanga praises God, the Holy One of the city of the saints, for God has shown mercy to His children and brought peace to humanity through the heavenly host, and He has saved us through the triumph of God, the Savior, the Lord of all, who said, 'Hosanna. Blessed is the King, the Son of David,' and they praised Him."

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Dama bo bu su má Nyɛmɛ ni, bɛrɛ sunsun sɔ saŋga kɛrɛ a. Am Si Nyɛmɛ si ka am koro nyinim n kɛrɛ.
translated: The child of Dama is also the child of Nyɛmɛ, and the young one follows the elder one. Then Nyɛmɛ said, "Come, let us go to the town."

ref: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
text: Am sere Nyɛmɛ ka má nyini sa n yó buke saŋga, wara Yuda­fɔm Ŋumi­yi cɛɛn ni.
translated: Am Sere Nyɛmɛ and his people went to the land of Judah, and the word of God came to him there.

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: Na fɛmɛ n akɔ, ka i kɔ niɛ jama ni, na wɔ ŋu ka sɔnɔ kun be wo jama n nu, i wura má konyaya sutura.
translated: Then he went to Akɔ, when he came and met the people there, and they went to seek the people's matter, he died and spoke the truth.

ref: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
text: Na wɔ kaaki ase bu ka, “M suse am wɔ lɛɛ, ka, m nú má ahin nzan bekun, má ka cɛɛn be bo m ni ambɛrɛ bá saŋga nu i fɔfɔrɛ, m Si Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n nu ni.”
translated: "And they said to him, 'I told you, I did not know that, but I will tell you what you should do, I Si Nyɛmɛ will help you in this matter.'"

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: Na ba bɔ i nu ka, ‘Be afa má ya ka ya yo i juma.’ Na wɔ se bu ka bɛrɛ gusu kɔ na bu kɔ yo juma i fie n su.
translated: And they said to him, "He has a demon and is raving mad." And they did not believe the word concerning the demon in their hearts.

ref: John's followers took his body and buried it. Then they told Jesus what had happened. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Mark
text: Na Yohane susu­fɔm n aba afa fuin n akɔ ase i, na ba kɔ ama Yesu labari. Kabo Yesu maari nbiɛsɔm akpii nnu ni bu minɛm diire ni
translated: And John baptized him in water and he came out from it, and he pointed out Jesus as the one. When Jesus arrived at the place to be baptized by him, he spoke words to him.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Dama kabo Jona yo woori jombaa bambaka be kunnu kɔ juuri le­san, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu ni, sɔ ye mini Adamande Wa ni bá wo asiɛn n bu kɔ ju le­san, kɔŋguɛ ni wiɛsu a.
translated: God told Jonah to go to the great city Nineveh, but he fled because the people there did not obey God, and he went to Tarshish instead.

ref: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
text: Saŋga bo bu bá faari am wuraari jɔrɛ­di­fɔm saa nu ni, má am ma am ahore saaki fa kɔ kabo am kɔ́ jɔjɔ barasu. Nyini saŋga n ba juuri, Nyɛmɛ má am nɔaniɛ ye am jɔjɔ a.
translated: When the elders speak, they speak with wisdom, and those who listen will find the right path. Let the people gather, for the Lord has called us to His house.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: I nati kɔɔri mɔ kaan bekun, na wɔ ŋu minɛ nnyɔ nbem. Bɛrɛ la Yakobo ni i niɛma Yohane. Bu fere bu si ka Zɛbɛdi. Bu wo lee nu suyo bu diɛndiɛm dɔŋgu.
translated: Jesus was walking beside the Sea of Galilee when he saw two brothers, Simon called Peter and Andrew. The sons of Jacob and his brother John. They were the sons of Zebedee. They were fishing and mending their nets.

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
text: Ambɛrɛ Kapɛnam­fɔm gusu, am sunsun ka Nyɛmɛ bá yɛ am su kɔ aŋgoro wɔ? Ai, i bá yo am kaan wɔ, na i fá am cɔɔndi kɔ Yiwee­fɔm Woo­biri nu. Alekutura sam bo m yo maari am ni, má nzɛn m yoori bu Sodom miɛ kpiri n nu, má kisa, miɛ n toko wo bɛrɛ haari ni nyuma.
translated: When the Lord Kapɛnamfɔm comes, will he find faith on earth? Yes, he will find some here, and he will gather them to Yiweefɔm Woobiri. Alekutura will come to save his people, but those who reject him will face destruction like Sodom, and they will be cast into eternal fire.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: Na yaa ati fɛmɛ n agaya, na wɔ ma bu afa nyini akɔɔ n akɔ anyi i saraka, ka i kɔ di wahara dɔ, haari fa kɔ ju saŋga bo i bá tɔ kaari n kɛrɛ wie.”
translated: When he went to the farm, he took a new path to the market, but there was a big problem when they entered the bush to hunt early in the morning.

ref: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
text: Na wɔ se bu ka, “Am fa bu ba ma m fɛn.”
translated: "And they said to the person, 'I will take another one to give me my own.'"

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo i yo Nyɛmɛ sa koroo­wa, yiri ti m siwa, ni m niwa, ni m ni a.”
translated: He said to them, "Nyɛmɛ has spoken, I have seen, I have heard, I have known."

ref: because he was teaching the disciples that the Son of Man would be handed over to people who would kill him. But three days later he would rise to life.
text: dama i sukere i susu­fɔm wɔ. Na wɔ se bu ka, “Bu bá tɔ mini Adamande Wa n ma minɛ nbem na bu kún m, ama, bu kuunri m wieeri, i le­san cɛɛn sin, na m téŋge m yiwee n nu.”
translated: The child is the one who eats sugar and dirt. And he said to him, “If you do not want to listen to Adamande Wa, who raised you and gave you life, then, you will suffer, you will be lost, and I will not care anymore.”

ref: They also had a few little fish, and after Jesus had blessed these, he told the disciples to pass them around.
text: Bu la jombaa kan kan nbem gusu, na wɔ fa ada Nyɛmɛ asi, na wɔ fa ama i susu­fɔm n ka bu fa nyini n gusu ma jama ni.
translated: They held a meeting to collect money, and they entrusted it to Nyɛmɛ, and they appointed him as the susu collector when they gave him the money to collect and keep safe.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
text: Ambɛrɛ Nyɛmɛ nɔaniɛ kere­fɔm, ni Farasi­fɔm n bo am ti burukɔɔ­nu­fɔm ni, am bá ŋu anyum­boro. Am ŋmisi am kɔpum ni taalɛm sin wɔ, ama, na bu kunnu ayi ni dekem bo am fin bu faŋga, ni anyii sa­yoo­wam wɔ.
translated: Jesus spoke to the Pharisees and the scribes, saying, "A man had two sons. The younger one said to his father, 'Father, give me my share of the estate.' So he divided his property between them. Not long after, the younger son gathered all his belongings and went to a distant country, where he squandered his wealth in reckless living. After he had spent everything, a severe famine arose in that country, and he began to be in need. So he went and hired himself out to a citizen of that place, who sent him to his fields to feed pigs. He longed to fill his stomach with the pods that the pigs were eating, but no one gave him anything. When he came to his senses, he said, 'How many of my father's hired workers have more than enough bread, but here I am dying of hunger! I will get up and go to my father, and I will say to him, "Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you. I am no longer worthy to be called your son; treat me like one of your hired workers."' So he got up and went to his father. But while he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was filled with compassion; he ran, threw his arms around him, and kissed him. The son said to him, 'Father, I have sinned against heaven and before you; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.' But the father said to his slaves, 'Quickly, bring the best robe and put it on him; put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet. Bring the fattened calf and kill it, and let us eat and celebrate; for this son of mine was dead and has come back to life; he was lost and has been found!' And they began to celebrate.

ref: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Nyɛmɛ fɛmɛya n ti ka sɔnɔ be bo i yasuuri alecin ŋgarama fuun, ka i kɔ niɛniɛ npa­di­fɔm na bu kɔ yo i fie juma n wɔ.
translated: And Jesus said to them, "Go and heal the sick, and proclaim peace to the villages, for those who welcome you will receive blessings."
